overview: Jund Gardens is a Pauper midrange deck that leverages land sacrifice and life gain to outlast opponents while applying steady pressure. By converting lands into card advantage and board wipes, it disrupts traditional mana bases and controls the pace of the game. The strategy relies on efficient removal, resilient creatures, and timely land destruction to close out matches.
win_conditions: Trading lands for life and card advantage to outvalue opponents
Using Defile to disrupt enemy mana while maintaining board presence
Sacrificing creatures with Fanatical Offering to stabilize and close out games

keep_criteria: Hands with early land drops and a removal spell
Combinations featuring Fanatical Offering and a creature to sacrifice
Defile paired with a way to play it on curve
Keep hands with 2-3 mana sources (including at least one Garden or equivalent mana rock) and a turn-1 or turn-2 play.
Prioritize hands with Fanatical Offering paired with a land, or a cheap threat/removal combo.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early land interaction or card draw
Overloaded hands with multiple Defiles and no creatures
Hands missing a clear path to turn two or three
Mulligan on 3+ lands with no early spells, 0-1 lands with no fixing, or hands with only high-cost spells and no disruption.

early_game: Play lands and cheap creatures to establish board presence
Use Fanatical Offering on turn two or three to gain life and remove threats
Hold Defile for when opponent commits to a large land drop
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Thoughtseize if holding a land drop to strip key interaction.
Turn 2: Play a second land, cast a cheap threat (e.g., Bloodtithe Harvester, Tarmogoyf) or use Fanatical Offering to cast a spell from the graveyard.
Sequence lands to ensure double-green drops
use Defile or spot removal only if it enables a lethal threat or removes a critical early blocker.
mid_game: Cycle lands into card advantage using Lembas and other sac outlets
Trade efficiently to keep the board clear while gaining life
Apply pressure with resilient creatures while waiting for opponent to run out of resources
Preserve the graveyard for high-value Fanatical Offering targets
avoid over-committing removal to low-impact threats.
Use Lembas for life gain or mana when pressured, but don't sacrifice it if you need the mana for a game-winning play.
Track hand size to avoid discarding key graveyard synergies or disruption spells.
Commit threats when the opponent has no interaction or is tapped out
hold back removal for key threats or combo pieces.
late_game: Use Defile to strip opponent's mana base if they are ahead
Sacrifice remaining lands and creatures to stabilize and close out
Rely on life total and card advantage to outlast aggressive strategies
Tarmogoyf, Bloodtithe Harvester, or a resolved Fanatical Offering targeting a high-value spell/creature.
Leverage graveyard recursion to maintain board presence and pressure.
Use Defile or spot removal to clear the path for lethal.
Go all-in when the opponent has no interaction, when you can safely cast a game-winning threat via Fanatical Offering, or when you have a clear path to lethal.
Commit aggressively against combo decks if you can disrupt their win condition or race them with resilient threats.

key_cards.name: Fanatical Offering
key_cards.notes: Use on opponent's threats or your own creatures to stabilize and gain tempo. Prioritize sacrificing high-power creatures for maximum life gain.

key_cards.name: Lembas
key_cards.notes: Sacrifice to draw a card when you need answers or to cycle into better resources. Keep it on the board to pressure opponents while waiting for removal.

key_cards.name: Defile
key_cards.notes: Cast when opponent has multiple lands untapped or after they play a key spell. Use it to disrupt mana while your board remains intact due to life gain and sac outlets.

sequencing_tips: Hold Defile until after opponent's main phase to maximize land disruption
Sacrifice creatures with Fanatical Offering before casting Defile to preserve life total
Use Lembas for card draw when you have a full board or need specific answers
Common mistake: Overusing Fanatical Offering on low-value targets early, leaving you without answers when the game slows down.
Remember that Garden's mana ability is a triggered ability, so it can be responded to
Lembas is crucial for survival against aggressive decks, but don't sacrifice it if you need the mana for a game-winning play.
if it resolves, pivot to a more direct game plan and avoid relying on graveyard recursion.

matchups.advice: Prioritize early Fanatical Offering to gain life and remove threats. Hold Defile for when they go wide.
Apply steady pressure and use Defile to disrupt their late-game mana. Sacrifice lands to draw cards and outvalue them.
Race them by leveraging life gain and land sacrifice. Use Defile to break their mana curve.

Use cheap, multiple threats to force counterspells and trade up. Cast spells via Fanatical Offering to bypass counters if possible, though counters still work. Use discard to remove key pieces and pressure through interaction.

Apply early pressure to interrupt their development. Use spot removal on key mana sources and bring in graveyard hate. Don't over-invest in the graveyard if they can exile it; pivot to direct pressure.

Win the discard war and prioritize keeping hand size. Use Graze/Thoughtseize to strip their key pieces and win via superior graveyard resources and efficient removal. Track their graveyard to avoid over-committing to Fanatical Offering targets.

common_threats: Watch for graveyard hate (Rest in Peace, Scavenging Ooze), counterspells, and board wipes.
Note when opponents tap out for lock pieces or big threats.
Track their graveyard size and hand composition to anticipate when they'll have interaction available.
